subtitle: Sharp outer knee pain, especially downhill

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Typically appears at a consistent point in long runs

mild.description: Outer knee tightness in the last few km of a run
mild.can_run_note: Yes — flat routes, stop if sharp pain develops
why: IT band friction over the lateral femoral condyle at the knee
Weak glute medius causing inward knee collapse
Too much downhill running or cambered road running
relief: Foam roll TFL and outer quad — not the IT band itself
Ice outer knee for 15 min after runs
Reduce long run distance by 30% temporarily
prevention: Clamshells and lateral band walks — glute medius is the key
Alternate road camber on out-and-back routes

moderate.description: Sharp lateral knee pain that forces you to stop mid-run
moderate.can_run_note: Short easy runs only — stop the moment sharp pain starts
why: Sustained IT band compression causing local bursa inflammation
Chronic glute weakness not addressed early enough
High weekly mileage without adequate hip strength base
relief: Daily hip strengthening: glute bridges, clamshells, monster walks
Deep tissue massage to TFL — not the knee area
prevention: Hip abductor strength programme — 3× per week minimum
Reduce downhill volume to under 10% of total running
Check running cadence — low cadence increases IT band stress

severe.description: Pain within the first km of any run, pain walking stairs
severe.can_run_note: No running — cycling is fine if pain-free
why: Chronic IT band compression with significant bursal inflammation
Underlying biomechanical fault requiring professional assessment
relief: Physiotherapy for manual therapy and targeted rehab
Corticosteroid injection may be considered for bursal inflammation
prevention: 8–12 week structured hip strengthening before return to running
Gradual return starting on treadmill with slight incline to reduce IT band tension
Gait analysis to identify and correct hip drop pattern

see_physio_if: Pain starts within the first km of running
Pain present when walking or climbing stairs
No improvement after 2 weeks of rest and hip strengthening
